wiki.content: "TV Off" (stylized in all lowercase) is the seventh track on Kendrick Lamar's sixth album, GNX, surprise-released on November 22, 2024 & released as the album's second single alongside squabble up 4 days after it's release on November 26, 2024. The song features fellow L.A. rapper Lefty Gunplay, & was produced by Sounwave, Jack Antonoff, Mustard, Kamasi Washington, Sean Momberger, and Larry "Larry Jayy" Sanders. The song marks a reunion with producer Mustard, who previously worked with Kendrick on the acclaimed diss track Not Like Us, which debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 earlier in 2024. This context sets the stage for TV Off which builds on the momentum of their prior success. The song reflects a call to action for individuals to rise above mediocrity, avoid toxic influences, and remain focused on their purpose. By metaphorically urging listeners to "turn the TV off", the song critiques passive consumption and conformity. Other themes include: authenticity, accountability, survival, and self-sufficiency. In a November 2024 interview with Billboard, Mustard explained that 2 halves of the beat were put together at different times, with the first half being adjacent to him making the now-famous "Not Like Us" beat, while the second half stemmed from a separate, non-Kendrick project. Apparently, this portion was originally intended to be part of a planned theatrical album of his own before Kendrick expressed interest in it. tv off debuted at it's peak of #2 on the Billboard Hot 100 following the release of GNX on the week of December 7, 2024 & uniquely rose in performance the following week, with it almost becoming Kendrick's at the time sixth #1 song if not in the way of rising Christmas music. The song spent 9 non-consecutive weeks in the top 10 (even with performance halted by rising Christmas music & potential) alongside other Kendrick songs such as squabble up, luther featuring SZA & additionally "Not Like Us" & his feature on SZA's 30 for 30. tv off ranked at #15 on Billboard's Year-End list of 2025. The song was nominated for Best Rap Performance and won Best Rap Song at the 2026 Grammy Awards. Read more on Last.fm.
